title: potentilla heptaphylla subsp. bouquoyana
intro: Potentilla heptaphylla subsp. bouquoyana, also known as the Seven-leaved Cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, Potentilla heptaphylla subsp. bouquoyana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
